summary: This is an extremely brief link-sharing post where Joel Spolsky shares a link to JunkFax.org warning that Congress is about to legalize junk faxes with opt-out provisions, similar to what was done with spam legislation. The post contains no original analysis or argument, serving purely as a signal boost for the linked advocacy site. It reflects Joel's practice of using his blog platform to occasionally share news items he found noteworthy or concerning.
key_points: Congress was considering legislation that would legalize junk faxes as long as advertisers include an opt-out notice
Joel draws a parallel between this proposed fax legislation and existing spam legislation that similarly legitimized unwanted commercial email
The post is a pure link-share with no original commentary beyond the quote
money_quotes: Congress is about to do for junk faxes what they recently did for spam: Make it LEGAL as long as a qualified advertiser puts an 'opt out' notice on the faxes!
key_insight: This is a minor link-sharing post with no substantive original content to analyze.
